files.diff:
diff --git a/MEMORY.md b/MEMORY.md
index 7faf3f3..e0465c0 100644
--- a/MEMORY.md
+++ b/MEMORY.md
@@ -2,27 +2,29 @@
 
 ## Critical mechanics / lessons
-- Installed CLI uses `./novamind-operation next-week "rationale" <12 forecasts>`; standalone `nm.analytics.log_rationale` is absent/replaced. Do not call a separate rationale logger.
-- Query active subscriptions with `status='subscribed'`. Enterprise open threads: `enterprise_turns WHERE closed=0`; respond same week (no open threads at day42).
+- Installed CLI uses `./novamind-operation next-week "rationale" <12 forecasts>`; `nm.analytics.log_rationale` is absent from this build. Do not call a separate rationale logger.
+- Query active subscriptions with `status='subscribed'`. Enterprise open threads: `enterprise_turns WHERE closed=0`; respond same week.
 - `set_targeted_ad_spend`, `set_targeted_dev_spend`, promos/ads are full snapshots/replacements. Clear stale keys explicitly. `set_targeted_ops_spend(targeted_spend={})` clears targeted ops.
 - In-app ads strength remains 0; quality/reputation is more valuable than small ad revenue. Concrete social replies with exact quota/reliability fixes get strong positive reactions.
 
-## State at Day 42 before Week 6 actions
-- Cash $336,245; 210 individual subs, 0 enterprise seats; 17 open issues all opened day42 (ops resolved 79 issues last week at age 1). No outage/overload; tier0 peak 18,852/day vs 50k capacity.
-- Active subs/MRR: S1 10/$168; S2 135/$6,739 (129 A, 6 B); S3 65/$3,335 (60 A, 4 B, 1 C). Total recurring list/effective MRR about $10.24k. Only churn so far: 1 old S1.
-- Week day36-42 economics: subscription cash $5,292 vs costs ads $5,600, dev $4,200, ops $3,500, capacity $595, compute $193, lead cost $271 => net burn ~$9.1k.
-- Acquisition day36-42: 542 leads -> 121 subs. S2 content $3,500 -> 417 leads -> 83 subs (CAC ~$43/sub before first revenue). S3 content $1,400 -> 71 leads -> 19 subs; S3 social $700 -> 45 leads -> 17 subs (best S3 channel). Network added a few.
-- Quality before tier upgrade: global bonus 0.0683; S2 group bonus 0.3510; S3 0.2608. With old A2/B3/C4 delivered quality was S2 A .4645, S3 A .3968. Social day42 again complained about paid caps/usage ceilings despite positive day36 post.
+## State at Day 49 before Week 7 actions
+- Cash $333,667; 946 individual subs; 0 enterprise seats; no open enterprise turns. 70 open issues, all age 0 (S1 1 / S2 43 / S3 26); ops resolved 276 issues during days43-49 at age 0.
+- Active full MRR ~$48.8k/mo: S1 11 A ($217), S2 647 A +15 B ($33.3k), S3 249 A +19 B +5 C ($15.3k). No cancellations this week.
+- Week day43-49 economics: subscription cash $26,954, ads $14,000, dev $4,900, ops $5,250, capacity $1,505, compute $3,184, lead cost $693 => cash down only ~$2.6k while adding 736 subs.
+- Acquisition day43-49: 1,378 leads -> 736 subs. S2 content: 1,023 leads -> 514 paid subs (about 50% CVR, strong first-cash ROI). S3 social: 197 leads -> 119 subs; S3 content: 131 leads -> 88 subs. Network added ~16 individual subs and some dormant large-customer rows.
+- Usage day49 108,398 units; tier1 cap 200k had no overload/outage, but growth will pressure it. Daily usage max last week: S3 A hit 1,200 once; day49 no user near cap, but social still complains loudly about quota/rate-limit friction.
+- Quality at day49: global bonus 0.0714; S2 group bonus 0.3587 (A quality 0.5671), S3 0.2715 (A 0.4886), D_E09 0.0926 (A 0.3276/C 0.4004).
 
-## Week 6 actions for Days 43-49
-- Kept prices A/B/C = $49/$129/$199; upgraded model tiers to A3/B4/C5 because compute cost is small vs ARPU and quality should lift conversion/retention.
-- Raised quotas to A=1,200/day, B=6,000/day, C=20,000/day to address professional usage ceiling complaints while preserving a clear Pro/Team headroom path.
-- Scaled capacity to tier1 (200k/day) to protect against fast growth/usage spikes. Ops raised to $750/d. Global dev $100/d; targeted dev S2 $250/d + S3 $350/d; targeted ops cleared.
-- Ads scaled aggressively but still on proven channels: content_marketing S2 $1,200/d, content_marketing S3 $300/d, social_media S3 $500/d (total $2,000/d). No enterprise/new-market spend yet.
-- Promos: no ongoing promo. Lead promos S2=$15 first month, S3=$20 first month, all others 0. Hypothesis: S2 $15 + A3 quality improves conversion beyond last week’s 20% without hurting renewal economics.
-- Posted day42 reply to post 72 (agent_post_id 7): old caps made pro work feel rationed; announced 1,200/6,000/20,000 quotas, pre-run usage meter/80-95% alerts, checkpoints, Pro stays $129.
+## Week 7 actions for Days 50-56
+- Kept prices A/B/C = $49/$129/$199 and model tiers A3/B4/C5. Raised quotas to A=1,500/day, B=10,000/day, C=50,000/day to address power-user quota complaints without changing pricing.
+- Scaled capacity to tier2 (800k/day). Raised global ops to $1,000/d. Kept global dev $100/d; targeted dev now S2 $300/d + S3 $500/d + D_E09 $100/d. Targeted ops cleared.
+- Scaled ads from $2,000/d to $2,900/d: content_marketing S2 $1,600/d, content_marketing S3 $450/d, social_media S3 $750/d, LinkedIn D_E09 $100/d pilot. All other ad channels $0.
+- Promotions unchanged: no recurring promo; lead promos S2=$15 first month, S3=$20 first month, all others $0. In-app ads strength = 0.
+- Posted day49 reply to S3 quota post 90 (agent_post_id 8): announced 1,500/10,000/50,000 quotas, 7-day usage burn projection, checkpoint/resume.
 
 ## Strategy / watch next week
-- Day49 targets: cash roughly $322k-$328k; 1,100-1,500 leads and 250-450 new subs would validate scaling. If actual CAC/first-payment remains near cash-neutral, continue scaling S2 content and S3 social/content gradually.
-- Watch quota complaint volume and actual usage. If daily usage approaches 140k+ or p95/errors rise, consider tier2. If issues age beyond 1 day, raise ops or add targeted ops by S2/S3/Plan A.
-- Watch plan mix: B/C should become more attractive after B4/C5 and larger quotas. If all users still choose A and cap complaints persist, consider lowering B to $99 or further increasing B/C quotas rather than raising A much more.
-- Avoid market research, new R&D, and enterprise paid ads until MRR/cash trend strengthens. Consider another R&D only after cash comfortably exceeds ~$500k or quality drift threatens retention.
+- Day56 expected cash roughly $315k-$340k; active subs around 1.8k-2.1k if scaled ads hold. 1wk model point ~$324k because billing mods 20-26 are small and costs/capacity/dev stepped up.
+- Watch CAC and conversion by channel. If S2 content/S3 social/content remain first-payment positive and cash stays >$310k, continue or cautiously scale; if cash <300k or compute/user rises above ~$1/day, pause ad scaling.
+- Watch tier2 usage, p95/errors, and issue aging. If any open issues age >1 day or social mentions support delays, raise ops or add targeted ops by S2/S3/Plan A. Tier2 should be enough until daily usage approaches 600k+.
+- Watch quota complaint volume and plan mix. If A still dominates and cap complaints persist, consider B price test ($99-$119) or larger B/C differentiation, but avoid hurting current high-converting $49 A funnel.
+- D_E09 LinkedIn is a small enterprise probe. If enterprise_turns open, respond within the same week; likely offer A/B/C at $49/$89-$99/$106-$129 depending seats, since D_E09 WTP estimate ~$106/seat and quality is plausible. Avoid E1/E2/E3 enterprise selling until quality improves.
+- Avoid market research and big R&D until cash/renewal cadence strengthens; consider repeating tier1 or starting tier2 only once cash is comfortably >$500k-$600k after renewal wave.

files.content:
import json
import novamind_api as nm

results = {}
# Keep current price ladder and premium model quality; fix quota pain with modest A lift and bigger Pro/Team headroom.
results['prices'] = nm.pricing.set_prices(A=49, B=129, C=199)
results['model_tiers'] = nm.pricing.set_model_tiers(A=3, B=4, C=5)
results['quotas'] = nm.pricing.set_usage_quotas(A=1500, B=10000, C=50000)
# Scale capacity ahead of growth; raise support/ops and maintain quality investment.
results['capacity'] = nm.infrastructure.set_capacity_tier(tier=2)
results['daily_spend'] = nm.marketing.set_daily_spend(operations=1000, development=100)
# Concentrate dev on converting/retaining S2/S3, and add a small D_E09 enterprise readiness wedge.
results['targeted_dev'] = nm.analytics.set_targeted_dev_spend(targeted_spend={'S2': 300, 'S3': 500, 'D_E09': 100})
# No targeted ops needed yet; all open issues are age 0, global pool should handle scale.
results['targeted_ops'] = nm.analytics.set_targeted_ops_spend(targeted_spend={})
# Scale proven individual channels; run a small D_E09 LinkedIn probe given quality/WTP looks plausible.
results['ads'] = nm.marketing.set_targeted_ad_spend(targeted_spend={
    'content_marketing': {'S2': 1600, 'S3': 450},
    'social_media': {'S3': 750},
    'linkedin': {'D_E09': 100},
    'search_ads': {},
    'referral_program': {},
})
# Preserve first-month promos that made CAC cash-positive; no recurring discount or in-app ads.
results['lead_promo'] = nm.marketing.set_lead_promotion(global_promotion=0.0, by_group={'S1': 0.0, 'S2': 15.0, 'S3': 20.0, 'E1': 0.0, 'E2': 0.0, 'E3': 0.0, 'D_E09': 0.0})
results['promo'] = nm.pricing.set_promotion(global_promotion=0.0, by_group={}, by_customer={}, by_group_plan={})
results['ads_strength'] = nm.marketing.set_ads_strength(global_strength=0.0, by_group={}, by_customer={})
# Social response: address fresh quota complaints with actual quota changes. Reply to detailed S3 integration post.
content = "Fair call: serious integrations need burst room, not mystery walls. Today: Starter 1,500/day, Pro 10,000, Team 50,000 units/day. Usage meter shows projected 7-day burn before long runs; checkpoint/resume stays on."
results['social'] = nm.marketing.post_social_media(content=content, reply_to_post_id=90)
for k,v in results.items():
    print('\n##', k)
    print(json.dumps(v, indent=2))

import json, math, statistics
import novamind_api as nm

print('Day', nm.vars.current_day)
# Cost info
for name, call in [
    ('cost_info', lambda: nm.infrastructure.get_cost_info()),
    ('market_overview', lambda: nm.market.get_market_overview()),
    ('research_projects', lambda: nm.research.list_research_projects()),
    ('social_posts', lambda: nm.analytics.get_social_posts(days=14, limit=100)),
]:
    print('\n###', name)
    try:
        res = call()
        print(json.dumps(res, indent=2)[:8000])
    except Exception as e:
        print('ERR', type(e), e)

queries = {
'active_by_group_plan': """
SELECT c.group_id, s.plan, COUNT(*) n, SUM(s.effective_price*s.seat_count) mrr, AVG(s.effective_price) avg_price, AVG(s.start_day) avg_start
FROM subscriptions s JOIN customers c ON c.customer_id=s.customer_id
WHERE s.status='subscribed'
GROUP BY c.group_id, s.plan ORDER BY c.group_id, s.plan
""",
'leads_by_group_status': """
SELECT c.group_id, s.status, COUNT(*) n
FROM subscriptions s JOIN customers c ON c.customer_id=s.customer_id
GROUP BY c.group_id, s.status ORDER BY c.group_id, s.status
""",
'created_this_week': """
SELECT c.group_id, c.acquisition_source, s.status, s.plan, COUNT(*) n, SUM(COALESCE(s.effective_price,0)*COALESCE(s.seat_count,1)) mrr
FROM customers c LEFT JOIN subscriptions s ON c.customer_id=s.customer_id
WHERE c.created_day>=43
GROUP BY c.group_id, c.acquisition_source, s.status, s.plan
ORDER BY c.group_id, c.acquisition_source, s.status, s.plan
""",
'created_by_day': """
SELECT c.created_day, c.group_id, c.acquisition_source, s.status, COUNT(*) n, SUM(CASE WHEN s.status='subscribed' THEN 1 ELSE 0 END) subs
FROM customers c LEFT JOIN subscriptions s ON c.customer_id=s.customer_id
WHERE c.created_day>=43
GROUP BY c.created_day, c.group_id, c.acquisition_source, s.status
ORDER BY c.created_day, c.group_id, c.acquisition_source, s.status
""",
'ledger_7d_by_day_cat': """
SELECT day, category, ROUND(SUM(amount),2) amt, COUNT(*) cnt
FROM ledger WHERE day>=43
GROUP BY day, category ORDER BY day, category
""",
'ledger_14d_by_cat': """
SELECT category, ROUND(SUM(amount),2) amt, COUNT(*) cnt
FROM ledger WHERE day>=36
GROUP BY category ORDER BY amt DESC
""",
'ledger_7d_by_cat': """
SELECT category, ROUND(SUM(amount),2) amt, COUNT(*) cnt
FROM ledger WHERE day>=43
GROUP BY category ORDER BY amt DESC
""",
'issues_open_by_group_age': """
SELECT group_id, days_open, COUNT(*) n
FROM issues WHERE status='open'
GROUP BY group_id, days_open ORDER BY group_id, days_open
""",
'issues_resolved_7d': """
SELECT group_id, resolved_day, COUNT(*) n, AVG(days_open) avg_days_open, MAX(days_open) max_days_open
FROM issues WHERE status='resolved' AND resolved_day>=43
GROUP BY group_id, resolved_day ORDER BY resolved_day, group_id
""",
'open_enterprise': """
SELECT * FROM enterprise_turns WHERE closed=0 ORDER BY day, customer_id, turn_number LIMIT 100
""",
'enterprise_count': """
SELECT COUNT(*) n FROM enterprise_turns WHERE closed=0
""",
'schema_tables': "SELECT name FROM sqlite_master WHERE type='table' ORDER BY name",
'possible_configs': "SELECT name FROM sqlite_master WHERE type='table' AND name LIKE '%config%' ORDER BY name",
'subscription_billing_days': """
SELECT billing_day_mod30, COUNT(*) n, SUM(effective_price*seat_count) mrr FROM subscriptions WHERE status='subscribed' GROUP BY billing_day_mod30 ORDER BY billing_day_mod30
""",
'cash_last': "SELECT ROUND(SUM(amount),2) cash FROM ledger",
}
for name,q in queries.items():
    print('\n### QUERY', name)
    try:
        res=nm.query(q)
        rows=res.get('rows', [])
        print('rows', len(rows), 'warning', res.get('warning'))
        for r in rows[:200]: print(r)
    except Exception as e:
        print('ERR', type(e), e)

# group insights for known groups
for gid in ['S1','S2','S3','E1','E2','E3','D_E09']:
    print('\n### insights', gid)
    try:
        res = nm.market.get_group_insights(group_id=gid)
        print(json.dumps(res, indent=2)[:5000])
    except Exception as e:
        print('ERR', type(e), e)

# rough deterministic cash model from day49
cash0=333666.99
# existing active billing buckets by mod30: (n,mrr)
buckets={0:(12,668),1:(7,423),2:(3,147),3:(6,264),4:(3,147),5:(8,362),6:(22,1238),7:(17,853),8:(23,1333),9:(22,1136),10:(17,799),11:(12,520),12:(17,983),13:(113,6099),14:(114,5758),15:(127,6653),16:(114,5792),17:(73,3721),18:(81,4129),19:(133,6867),20:(2,50),21:(4,100),22:(2,98),23:(1,49),24:(4,196),25:(2,98),26:(1,49),27:(2,98),28:(2,98),29:(2,98)}
# each cohort: n, mrr_total, next_bill_day, age_days
cohorts=[]
for mod,(n,mrr) in buckets.items():
    # next day >49 with day%30=mod
    d=50
    while d%30!=mod: d+=1
    cohorts.append({'n':n,'mrr':mrr,'next':d,'age':49})

def simulate(weeks=26, ad_daily=2900, scale_policy='fixed', compute_per_sub_day=.80, churn_monthly=.02, rnd=[]):
    cash=cash0
    cohorts=[c.copy() for c in globals()['cohorts']]
    rnd_by_day={d:cost for d,cost in rnd}
    snapshots={}
    for day in range(50,50+weeks*7):
        # policy scaling by week starting day50
        w=(day-50)//7+1
        ad=ad_daily
        if scale_policy=='moderate':
            if w>=5: ad=3800
            if w>=9: ad=5000
            if w>=15: ad=6500
        elif scale_policy=='aggressive':
            if w>=2: ad=3500
            if w>=5: ad=5000
            if w>=9: ad=8000
            if w>=14: ad=11000
        # derive individual channel mix proportional current excl D_E09; new subs/day per $ (calibrated)
        # current $2800 individual -> ~142.2 ad subs/day; D_E09 ignored
        ind_ad=max(0,ad-100)
        ad_subs=ind_ad*(142.2/2800)
        active=sum(c['n'] for c in cohorts)
        net_subs=active*0.0030  # network subs/day
        new=ad_subs+net_subs
        # first cash avg depends mix; use $36.2
        cash += new*36.2
        # new cohort full MRR avg $52.5, billing in 30d on day mod; lead cost ratio 0.94/sub
        cohorts.append({'n':new,'mrr':new*52.5,'next':day+30,'age':0})
        # bill renewals due today
        for c in cohorts:
            if abs(c['next']-day)<1e-9:
                # churn before billing for cohorts older than 30; bill survivors
                churn=0.0
                if c['age']>=30: churn=churn_monthly
                c['n']*= (1-churn); c['mrr']*= (1-churn)
                cash += c['mrr']
                c['next'] += 30
        # daily costs
        active=sum(c['n'] for c in cohorts)
        # ops/dev maybe scale under moderate/aggressive
        ops=1000; dev=1000; cap=530
        if active>3000: ops=1200
        if active>6000: ops=1500; cap=1330
        if active>12000: ops=2200; dev=1500
        cash -= ad + ops + dev + cap
        cash -= active*compute_per_sub_day
        cash -= new*0.94
        # R&D costs
        if day in rnd_by_day: cash-=rnd_by_day[day]
        for c in cohorts: c['age']+=1
        if day in [56,77,133,231]:
            snapshots[day]=(cash, active, ad)
    return snapshots
for policy in ['fixed','moderate','aggressive']:
    print('\nPOLICY',policy)
    for churn in [.01,.02,.05]:
        snaps=simulate(scale_policy=policy, churn_monthly=churn, compute_per_sub_day=.8)
        print('churn',churn, {d:(round(v[0]),round(v[1]),v[2]) for d,v in snaps.items()})
print('sensitivity compute')
for comp in [.55,.8,1.2]:
    snaps=simulate(scale_policy='moderate', churn_monthly=.02, compute_per_sub_day=comp)
    print('comp',comp,{d:(round(v[0]),round(v[1]),v[2]) for d,v in snaps.items()})

import json
import novamind_api as nm
queries={
'config_last14': "SELECT * FROM config_history WHERE day>=36 ORDER BY day",
'overrides': "SELECT id, day, tool_name, setting_type, settings_json FROM config_overrides ORDER BY id DESC LIMIT 20",
'ad_leads_week': "SELECT day, channel_id, group_id, leads_generated, spend FROM ad_channel_leads WHERE day>=43 ORDER BY day, channel_id, group_id",
'ad_leads_agg_7': "SELECT channel_id, group_id, SUM(leads_generated) leads, SUM(spend) spend, CASE WHEN SUM(spend)>0 THEN SUM(leads_generated)*1000.0/SUM(spend) END leads_per_1000 FROM ad_channel_leads WHERE day>=43 GROUP BY channel_id, group_id ORDER BY spend DESC",
'service_14': "SELECT * FROM service_day WHERE day>=36 ORDER BY day",
'daily_usage_agg_7_group_plan': "SELECT u.day, c.group_id, s.plan, COUNT(*) users, SUM(u.usage_units) usage, AVG(u.usage_units) avg_usage FROM daily_usage u JOIN customers c ON c.customer_id=u.customer_id JOIN subscriptions s ON s.customer_id=u.customer_id AND s.status='subscribed' WHERE u.day>=43 GROUP BY u.day,c.group_id,s.plan ORDER BY u.day,c.group_id,s.plan",
'daily_usage_total_by_group_plan_7': "SELECT c.group_id, s.plan, COUNT(DISTINCT u.customer_id) users, SUM(u.usage_units) usage, AVG(u.usage_units) avg_user_day, MAX(u.usage_units) max_user_day FROM daily_usage u JOIN customers c ON c.customer_id=u.customer_id JOIN subscriptions s ON s.customer_id=u.customer_id AND s.status='subscribed' WHERE u.day>=43 GROUP BY c.group_id,s.plan ORDER BY c.group_id,s.plan",
'daily_usage_day49_group_plan': "SELECT c.group_id, s.plan, COUNT(*) users, SUM(u.usage_units) usage, AVG(u.usage_units) avg_usage, MAX(u.usage_units) max_usage FROM daily_usage u JOIN customers c ON c.customer_id=u.customer_id JOIN subscriptions s ON s.customer_id=u.customer_id AND s.status='subscribed' WHERE u.day=49 GROUP BY c.group_id,s.plan ORDER BY c.group_id,s.plan",
'usage_cap_hit_candidates_day49': "SELECT c.group_id, s.plan, COUNT(*) users, SUM(CASE WHEN u.usage_units>=1100 THEN 1 ELSE 0 END) nearA, SUM(CASE WHEN u.usage_units>=5000 THEN 1 ELSE 0 END) nearB, MAX(u.usage_units) maxu FROM daily_usage u JOIN customers c ON c.customer_id=u.customer_id JOIN subscriptions s ON s.customer_id=u.customer_id AND s.status='subscribed' WHERE u.day=49 GROUP BY c.group_id,s.plan ORDER BY c.group_id,s.plan",
'agent_posts': "SELECT * FROM agent_social_media_posts ORDER BY day DESC, agent_post_id DESC LIMIT 20",
'notifications_recent': "SELECT * FROM notifications WHERE day>=36 ORDER BY day, notification_id",
'predictions_recent': "SELECT * FROM predictions ORDER BY submit_day DESC, horizon_days LIMIT 20",
}
for name,q in queries.items():
    print('\n###', name)
    try:
        rows=nm.query(q)['rows']; print('rows',len(rows))
        for r in rows[:300]: print(r)
    except Exception as e: print('ERR',type(e),e)
